context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the create-service-object instructions from the published Rails Agent Skills tile.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: EVERY service object MUST have its test written and validated BEFORE implementation.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Write the spec for .call (with contexts for success, error, edge cases)

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Run the spec — verify it fails because the service does not exist yet

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: `.call` MUST return EXACTLY one of these two shapes — no other top-level keys, never a Boolean, raw model, or ActiveRecord object:

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: When no instance state is needed — use ONLY class methods, no `initialize`, no instance variables. Validators and stateless helpers should always use this pattern:
